channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: IEM Cologne 2026 Major Tournament Guide—Schedule, Format, and Teams
item.content:encoded:
The IEM Cologne 2026 Major is upon us. On June 2, Cologne will host its first Major in 10 years, acting as the culmination of the first half of the 2026 Counter-Strike calendar.

Insider Gaming has all the info you need to enjoy the IEM Cologne 2026 Major, including when each team enters the competition, where to watch the Major, and when crowds will join the action.

IEM Cologne 2026 Major Schedule and Format

The IEM Cologne 2026 Major features three Swiss stages, followed by an eight-team single-elimination, winner-takes-all bracket in the Lanxess Arena. The dates for the stages are:

  • Stage 1: June 2-5
  • Stage 2: June 6-9
  • Stage 3: June 11-15
  • Playoffs: June 18-21

Following an announcement from ESL in February, Stage 3 of the IEM Cologne 2026 Major will feature no Best of 1s, but Best of 1s remain for Stages 1 and 2. The format for the Swiss stages and Playoffs is as follows:

Stage 1 & 2:

  • 16-team Swiss stage
  • Elimination and promotion matches are Best of 3
  • Other matches are Best of 1
  • Three wins to advance to the next stage; three losses means elimination
  • Eight teams progress; eight are eliminated

Stage 3:

  • 16 Team Swiss stage
  • All matches Best of 3
  • Three wins to advance to the Playoffs; three losses means elimination.

Playoffs:

  • Eight-team single-elimination bracket
  • All matches are Best of 3, except for the Best of 5 Grand Final

IEM Cologne 2026 Major Teams

The teams for the IEM Cologne 2026 Major are:

Stage 3:

  • Vitality (EU)
  • Natus Vincere (EU)
  • Parivision (EU)
  • Furia (Americas)
  • Aurora (EU)
  • Falcons (EU)
  • MOUZ (EU)
  • The MongolZ (Asia)

Stage 2:

  • Spirit (EU)
  • Astralis (EU)
  • G2 (EU)
  • FUT (EU)
  • 9z (Americas)
  • paiN (Americas)
  • Monte (EU)
  • Legacy (Americas)

Stage 1:

  • GamerLegion (EU)
  • BIG (EU)
  • BetBoom (EU)
  • B8 (EU)
  • Heroic (EU)
  • Sinners (EU)
  • M80 (Americas)
  • Tyloo (Asia)
  • NRG (Americas)
  • Sharks (Americas)
  • Gaimin Gladiators (Americas)
  • MIBR (Americas)
  • Liquid (Americas)
  • Lynn Vision (Asia)
  • THUNDER dOWNUNDER (Asia)
  • FlyQuest (Asia)

The opening matches for Stage 1 of the IEM Cologne 2026 Major are:

  • GamerLegion vs NRG
  • B8 vs TYLOO
  • Heroic vs Sharks
  • BetBoom vs Gaimin Gladiators
  • BIG vs Liquid
  • M80 vs Lynn Vision
  • MIBR vs THUNDER dOWNUNDER
  • Sinners vs FlyQuest

Prize Pool

The total prize pool for the IEM Cologne 2026 Major is $1,250,000. First place earns the team $500,000 directly.

Revenue share for sticker sales post-major is based on performance in the major, meaning a good performance here can earn more than just prize money.

IEM Cologne 2026 Major Location

The IEM Cologne Major will take place in Cologne, Germany, featuring two venues where fans will be able to watch the action. From Stage 3, fans will join the tournamnet at the Cologne Palladium before Counter-Strike returns to its ancestral home in the Lanxess Arena for the playoffs.

  • Stage 3 venue: Cologne Palladium, Cologne
  • Playoffs: Lanxess Arena, Cologne

How to Watch the IEM Cologne Major 2026

The Cologne 2026 Major will be streamed through ESL’s official Counter-Strike YouTube, Twitch, and Kick streams. Twitch will also utilize its B stream for Group Stage matches:

  • ESLCS Kick
  • ESL Counter-Strike YouTube
  • ESLCS Twitch
  • ESLCSb Twitch

IEM Cologne 2026 Major Predictions

For the IEM Cologne 2026 Major, Insider Gaming will provide detailed views into each stage, with predictions of where teams will land in the Pick’Ems placements. You can see our IEM Cologne 2026 Stage 1 predictions here!

Looking further into the tournament, there is no doubt that Vitality will be the favorites to claim the Cologne trophy, which would be their third consecutive Major victory after Austin and Budapest wins.

That would equal Astralis’ three in a row from the FACEIT London Major to the StarLadder Berlin 2019 Major. Additionally, Vitality would equal Astralis’ record of four organization majors.

Whether an upset is possible will be something we will look at in more detail when teams like Spirit (Stage 2) and NAVI (Stage 3) enter the event, so keep your eyes peeled for our previews.

Let us know who you think will win the IEM Cologne 2026 Major on Insider Gaming’s Discord!
For other news, see the details of Insider Gaming’s Showcase!

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/iem-cologne-2026-major-tournament-guide/

item.title: Best Sony PlayStation Days of Play 2026 Deals: End Date & Sale Games
item.content:encoded:
PlayStation is bringing major savings and deals back into the fray in the Days of Play 2026 event, with a host of savings to enjoy across almost everything you could ever need.

Sony is going all-out in a busy few weeks for PlayStation, headlined by the latest PlayStation State of Play next week that will offer a fresh look at Marvel’s Wolverine, among other things, and plenty more big announcements are on the horizon.

Players can get involved in the celebrations in PlayStation’s Days of Play event, and Insider Gaming has picked out some of the best deals available.

Best Sony PlayStation Days of Play 2026 Deals

Major deals are available across pretty much everything you could need as a PlayStation gamer in the Days of Play deals, from new games, hardware, and PS Plus membership.

Showstopping titles like Ghost of Yotei, Marvel’s Spider-Man 2, and Resident Evil Requiem are all available with hefty discounts compared to their MSRP price, while if you’re in the hunt for a new console, picking up a Certified Refurbished Console is a great way to counter the growing price rises in the industry.

  • More Insider Gaming: PlayStation Patent Could Address Loading Screen Boredom

Check out the table below for the best deals we’ve spotted in PlayStation’s Days of Play 2026, including how much savings are available.

When Do PlayStation Days of Play Deals End?

The PlayStation Days of Play Deals are set to come to an end on Wednesday, June 10, 2026, though the exact time and date depend on your region in the world.

A countdown timer is available to check on the PlayStation website, showcasing exactly how many days, hours, minutes, and seconds there are to take advantage of the deals before they disappear, and it’s also the best place to keep track of exactly what deals are available.

  • More Insider Gaming: PlayStation Plus June’s Monthly Games and Game Catalog Additions Revealed

With the deals available for a few more weeks, the good news is there’s no race to take advantage of the bargains that are available, and if you need to wait until your next payday, you’ve got the time to do so.

Check out the table below for exactly when the 2026 Days of Play Deals end in major locations worldwide.

Which deal do you think is the best? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

Don’t forget to tune into the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28, featuring 55 total games, including 12 World Premieres.

MOBILE APP

item.title: It’s Time For Destiny 2 To End, But Bungie Must Make Destiny 3
item.content:encoded:
For as long as I can remember, Destiny has been part of my gaming life.

I’ve poured thousands of hours into Destiny and Destiny 2, experiencing nearly every high and low imaginable: The original loot cave, The Taken King, Curse of Osiris, Forsaken, sunsetting, Lightfall, The Final Shape, and now the beginnings of the Fate saga.

Every time I thought I was done with Destiny, Bungie found a way to pull me back in. That’s why the recent news surrounding Destiny 2 hit harder than I expected.

When it became clear Destiny 2 was effectively finished, it felt bittersweet. The game has been there through so many stages of my life, from discovering the original Destiny at 13 to now being in my mid-twenties. Some of my favorite gaming memories came from late-night raids and going Flawless in Trials with friends.

Honestly, I was okay with it ending. After nearly a decade, Destiny 2 felt like it had reached its natural conclusion with The Final Shape. It felt like the perfect moment to look ahead to Destiny 3.

Instead, reports suggest Destiny 3 isn’t even in development, and that’s unforgivable.

Destiny 2 Was Never Meant To Last Forever

After nearly a decade, Destiny 2 began to feel weighed down by its own history. The onboarding experience is confusing for new players, PvP has felt neglected for years, and content vaulting remains one of Bungie’s most controversial decisions.

Even as someone who loves the franchise, I can admit Destiny 2 went through rough periods. Over time, it became harder to ignore the feeling that the game was stretching beyond what it was built to handle.

  • More Insider Gaming: Subnautica 2 Early Access Review

That’s why The Final Shape felt so important. It wrapped up a decade-long saga in a satisfying way and felt like the perfect opportunity for Bungie to reset the franchise while Destiny was still standing on top.

That’s what Destiny 3 could have brought back. Instead, Bungie’s focus shifted toward Marathon.

Marathon Has Inevitably Hurt Destiny

If I’m being honest, I think I resent Marathon to some degree.

Not the developers or even the game itself. I actually reviewed Marathon positively and defended it when it was “cool” to hate the game online—I was even called a Sony shill!

  • More Insider Gaming: Forza Horizon 6 Review

I genuinely think something is interesting there. The gunplay is incredible, the atmosphere is strong, and mechanically, it still feels unmistakably Bungie.

But Marathon’s existence has undeniably impacted the future of Destiny.

For years, Destiny 2 has felt like a game slowly being stripped for parts. Reports pointed toward developers and resources being moved to Marathon, including much of the PvP team. Then came the layoffs following The Final Shape, making it difficult to ignore the feeling that Destiny was no longer Bungie’s priority.

You can feel it in the game itself. PvP stagnated, content cadence weakened, and the narrative introduced plot threads that now seemingly lead nowhere.

When player counts dropped, people used that as proof nobody wanted Destiny anymore. I don’t think that’s true at all.

Players didn’t suddenly stop loving Destiny’s universe; many simply became exhausted with a live-service model that increasingly felt directionless while Bungie’s attention shifted elsewhere.

Destiny Fans Aren’t Done With The Franchise

Despite everything, Destiny still has a level of loyalty most live-service games could only dream of.

The final update, Monument of Triumph, arrives on June 9, and fans are already planning to overload the servers one last time to show Bungie that the community is still there.

  • More Insider Gaming: What’s Wrong With Mixtape? Why Does the Internet Seem To Hate This Smash Hit?

Of course, player numbers will fall afterwards—the game is ending, after all. But this isn’t about proving people want to endlessly grind Destiny 2 forever; it’s about proving the fanbase still exists and wants more from the franchise itself.

Then there’s the petition calling for Destiny 3, which you can sign for yourself here, and it’s nearing 300,000 signatures. Sure, signing a petition is easy, but that isn’t really the point. What matters is the sentiment behind it. Hundreds of thousands of people cared enough to say they aren’t done with Destiny yet.

Even now, during one of Destiny 2’s darkest periods, with new content endlessly delayed just to be cancelled, its player numbers still surpass Marathon. At the time of writing, Destiny 2 has a 24-hour peak of 18,000, surpassing Marathon’s 12,000—and that’s only looking at Steam.

This is all despite Marathon being a relatively fresh release with a new season on the horizon, and again, I want to stress that I like Marathon quite a bit! But unfortunately, it will never hold the same emotional attachment or long-term investment that Destiny has built over an entire decade.

That’s also why tensions online have become so volatile. Some Destiny fans actively root for Marathon to fail because they blame it for Destiny’s decline. I don’t agree with attacking the game or its developers, but after years of watching Destiny stagnate while Bungie’s focus shifted elsewhere, the frustration is understandable.

In an ideal world, Bungie could fully support both franchises. But with layoffs, financial struggles, and Destiny increasingly feeling deprioritised, it’s hard to shake the feeling that Bungie made its choice—and it chose Marathon.

Sony Is Making A Huge Mistake

That’s why I think Sony is making a huge mistake by not pursuing Destiny 3.

Not because Destiny 2 should continue forever, but because Destiny as a franchise still has enormous potential. A sequel could provide a fresh onboarding experience, new systems, new enemy factions, and most importantly, a reason for players to feel excited again instead of exhausted.

Because while I may be done with Destiny 2, I’m still desperate for more Destiny.

And I think Sony and Bungie are dangerously close to misunderstanding the difference.

Do you think Destiny 3 needs to happen? Or do you wish Destiny 2 could live forever with new content and updates? Or am I hanging onto the past too much and need to find a new game? Let me know your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server!

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

item.title: Fortnite is Showing 10 Minutes of The Mandalorian and Grogu For Free
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite players are being shown the first 10 minutes of the upcoming Star Wars movie, The Mandalorian, and Grogu.

Epic Games’ popular battle royale title, Fortnite, is getting an update, v40.41, going live today, and players will experience some downtime on May 28. The update is set to bring Community Day, which will be hosted on May 30. However, players weren’t aware the game had a huge surprise in store for them, as they wait for the final update of the season to go live.

First 10 Minutes of The Mandalorian and Grogu Premieres in Fortnite

A new post on Fortnite’s blog reveals a Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu watch party island where players can watch the first 10 minutes of the upcoming movie. The watch party takes players to an interactive Nevarro-themed island where players will be asked to complete quests while fending off Imperial troops, bounty hunters, and more. They have also created a theatre-themed setup where players can sit and enjoy the first 10 minutes of the movie, followed by a Q&A with Jon Favreau.

  • Island Code: 9477-7400-8437

The Mandalorian and Grogu movie was released on May 22, 2026. The movie has been directed by Jon Favreau and co-written by Dave Filoni, and it comes as a replacement for the fourth season of the series. The goal for the duo in this season is to find the warlord Commander Coin. To do so, they need to first head to planet Shakari to rescue Jabba’s son and heir, Rotta. Mando has also received the Razor Crest ship as pre-payment. Have you watched the new Star Wars movie?

In other news, here are all the start time and date details for the Fortnite Live Event bringing an end to Chapter 7, Season 2. Additionally, Epic Games plans to add a major perk to Fortnite Crew in the near future, giving subscribers access to Disney+. What are your thoughts on the first 10 minutes of The Mandalorian and Grogu movie being shown in Fortnite? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Hollow Knight Silksong’s physical edition details surfacing online. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Hollow Knight Silksong Physical Edition Details Surface
item.content:encoded:
A new report has provided players with the release date, price, and contents of the Hollow Knight Silksong physical edition.

Earlier this year, outlets claimed that the highly revered title would receive a physical release in 2026. We have yet to receive an official announcement from the developers, Team Cherry, and based on the information in the latest report, it seems this version of the game will take a bit more time but will arrive in 2026. Learn about everything that has surfaced about the physical edition for Silksong below:

Hollow Knight Silksong Physical Edition Release Date Set for October 16, 2026

Through the latest report from billbil-kun on Dealabs, details about the physical edition of Hollow Knight: Silksong have been revealed, including the release date and price. The physical edition is set for release in the United States on October 16, 2026. It will be available for Switch 2, which is also compatible with the original Switch, along with PS5, Xbox Series X, and Xbox One versions.

In terms of pricing, the Switch 2 version of the game will cost $44.99, while the PS5, Xbox Series X, and Xbox One versions will be available for $39.99. The Switch 2 version will come with the full game on cartridge, featuring an upgrade specifically for the Switch 2. Additionally, fans will receive a 32-page manual and a fold-out map of Pharloom. The physical edition is being distributed by Fangamer, who also handled distribution for the previous title.

The report indicates that Hollow Knight: Silksong will receive a physical re-release for the Switch 2 and PS5 in the United States. This version is compatible with the Switch and will be available on October 16, 2026, priced at $34.99 for both platforms. The physical copy will include a game cartridge or disc, a 28-page manual, and a fold-out map of Hollownest. The date for the official announcement and details regarding Europe have not yet been revealed.

In other news, Hollow Knight: Silksong was voted as the Insider Gaming Community’s 2025 Indie Game of the Year. Additionally, here is the release period information for the Sea of Sorrows DLC. What are your thoughts on the physical edition details for Hollow Knight Silksong? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Mina the Hollower becoming a contender for indie GOTY in 2026. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
